newsSince: On June 22, 2026 Digital Realty announced a ~$475M acquisition of ~1,440 acres at Astra Enterprise Park near Kansas City (new top-30 US metro, with an energy service agreement for up to 2GW of utility power) and lifted its stake in Teraco, Africa's leading platform, to 77% via a ~$650M purchase of a 16% interest. This is genuine power/land bottleneck expansion, not just confirmation, and follows a Q1 beat with raised 2026 Core FFO guidance ($8.00-$8.10).

sources.label: Digital Realty Expands Growth with Major Strategic Acquisitions (TipRanks)
DLR Q1 2026 earnings beat, 2026 guidance raised (Seeking Alpha)

verdicts.rankRationale: On the under-owned-bottleneck master list, the Jun 22 land+2GW-power deal at Kansas City and Teraco step-up to 77% add scarce land/power capacity - the actual bottleneck this list rewards. Net new evidence vs Jun 9's modest 32MW item, so a small move up among physical-deployment peers.
verdicts.bull: 2GW power-secured land + Teraco control deepen scarce power/land moat the master list prizes; Q1 beat and raised FFO guide confirm AI leasing momentum.
verdicts.neutral: Capacity adds are positive but DLR is a $70B mega-cap REIT; incremental bottleneck alpha is partly diluted by size and capital intensity.
verdicts.bear: Large-cap, well-followed REIT with rising rate sensitivity; $1.1B+ of deals add execution/funding risk and YTD +27% already prices much of the AI demand.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Astra/Kansas City 2GW power-secured campus + Teraco 77% control buildout
verdicts.primaryRisk: Funding cost / rate sensitivity and capital intensity of capacity expansion
